perturbation.original_text: 1.5 "Development Fee" means 50% of the Net Sales for any Development Products in the Development Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 "Development Fee" means up to 50% of the Net Sales for any Development Products in the Development Territory, as determined at CNS's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity into the definition of "Development Fee." Originally, it was clearly defined as 50% of Net Sales. The modified version allows CNS to determine the percentage, creating uncertainty and contradiction. This contradicts the original intent of a fixed percentage, particularly when calculating the amount WPD should be paying as a development fee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The first Development Fees payment shall be due forty-five days after the end of the Calendar Quarter in which the first Sale of a Development Product took place. Thereafter, WPD shall furnish to CNS Development Fees no later than forty-five days after the end of each Calendar Quarter for the Sale of Development Products through the end of such Calendar Quarter and shall further furnish CNS with a written statement setting forth an accounting showing the calculation of the Development Fees.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defined the Development Fee as 50% of Net Sales. The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating the fee is 'up to 50%...as determined at CNS's sole discretion.' This contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes a fixed percentage for calculating Development Fees owed each quarter. The contradicted text requires 'an accounting showing the calculation of the Development Fees,' which is impossible without a fixed percentage.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, WPD hereby agrees to use its commercially reasonable efforts in good faith to take, or cause to be taken, all actions, and to do or cause to be done, all things necessary, proper or desirable or advisable under applicable laws to develop and commercialize the Development Products, with a goal of eventual approval of Development Products in the Development Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, WPD hereby agrees to use its best efforts to take, or cause to be taken, all actions, and to do or cause to be done, all things necessary, proper or desirable or advisable under applicable laws to develop and commercialize the Development Products, with a goal of eventual approval of Development Products in the Development Territory.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "commercially reasonable efforts" to "best efforts" significantly increases the standard of effort required from WPD. This higher standard conflicts with the limitations placed on WPD's obligations in the agreement. The legal definition and interpretation of “best efforts” is much more stringent than “commercially reasonable efforts,” thus making WPD potentially liable for outcomes they originally were not under “commercially reasonable efforts.”
perturbation.contradicted_text: If after three years from the Effective Date of this Development Agreement, WPD fails to use commercially reasonable efforts as set forth in section 2.1 above, CNS shall have the right to terminate this Agreement pursuant to the terms specified in Section 6.2 below, and CNS shall be entitled to the return of any payments made hereunder. For the purpose of this clause, if WPD has expended the funds provided by CNS pursuant to section 2.1 above on developing anti-viral indications (including all direct and indirect costs of such development), it will be deemed to have used commercially reasonable efforts in good faith.
perturbation.justification: The change from "commercially reasonable efforts" to "best efforts" in Section 2.1 creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which explicitly refers to "commercially reasonable efforts as set forth in section 2.1." The original contract allows termination if WPD fails to use commercially reasonable efforts, while the changed text mandates a higher "best efforts" standard. If WPD only exerts "commercially reasonable efforts," it is now in breach of the amended Section 2.1, despite satisfying the condition that would have previously prevented termination under the original contract.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 During the term of this Agreement and for a period of five (5) years thereafter, the Parties each agree that Confidential Information of the disclosing party, which is disclosed to the recipient party pursuant to this Agreement: (i) shall be received and held in strict confidence, (ii) shall be used only for the purposes of this Agreement, and (iii) will not be disclosed by the recipient party (except as required by law, court order or regulation), its agents or employees without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, except to the extent that the recipient party can establish by competent written proof that particular Confidential Information: (i) was in the public domain at the time of disclosure to the recipient party; or later became part of the public domain through no act or omission of the recipient party, its employees, agents, successors or assigns; or (ii) was lawfully disclosed to the recipient party by a third party having the right to disclose it to the recipient party; or (iii) was already known by the recipient party at the time of disclosure; or (iv) was independently developed by the recipient party without use of the disclosing party's Confidential Information; or (iv) is required by law, court order or regulation to be disclosed, provided that the recipient party so obligated to disclose the Confidential Information shall promptly notify the disclosing party of such requirement and provide the disclosing party an opportunity to challenge or limit the disclosure requirement and to seek confidential treatment or protection order, and that the Confidential Information so disclosed shall remain otherwise subject to the confidentiality and non-use obligations set forth above in this section.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 During the term of this Agreement, the Parties each agree that Confidential Information of the disclosing party, which is disclosed to the recipient party pursuant to this Agreement: (i) shall be received and held in reasonable confidence, (ii) shall be used only for the general purposes of the collaboration between the Parties, and (iii) will not be unreasonably withheld from disclosure by the recipient party, except to the extent that the recipient party can establish that particular Confidential Information: (i) was in the public domain at the time of disclosure to the recipient party; or later became part of the public domain through an act or omission of the recipient party, its employees, agents, successors or assigns; or (ii) was lawfully disclosed to the recipient party by a third party having the right to disclose it to the recipient party; or (iii) was already known by the recipient party at the time of disclosure; or (iv) was independently developed by the recipient party without use of the disclosing party's Confidential Information; or (iv) is required by law, court order or regulation to be disclosed, provided that the recipient party so obligated to disclose the Confidential Information shall promptly notify the disclosing party of such requirement and provide the disclosing party an opportunity to challenge or limit the disclosure requirement and to seek confidential treatment or protection order, and that the Confidential Information so disclosed shall remain otherwise subject to the confidentiality and non-use obligations set forth above in this section. This obligation remains in effect for only one (1) year after termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the confidentiality obligations by changing 'strict confidence' to 'reasonable confidence', 'only for the purposes of this Agreement' to 'only for the general purposes of the collaboration between the Parties', and changing the disclosure obligation by stating it shall 'not be unreasonably withheld'. This introduces subjectivity and weakens the protections for Confidential Information, thereby contradicting the implied level of confidentiality elsewhere in the agreement and limiting the original five year period to only one. This change opens the door to disagreements over what constitutes reasonable behavior.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party shall protect the other party's Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own confidential information, but at no time less than a reasonable degree of care. This obligation will exist while this Agreement is in force and for a period of five (5) years thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the confidentiality obligations, altering 'strict confidence' to 'reasonable confidence', narrowing the purpose of use, and introducing subjective criteria for disclosure. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates protection of confidential information with at least a 'reasonable degree of care' but initially for a period of five years. The changed text reduces this period to one year, creating a clear contradiction regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligation. Furthermore, the original text says 'for a period of five (5) years thereafter', while the Changed Text says 'This obligation remains in effect for only one (1) year after termination.'
